note_data.title: Dialogue Writing (مکالمہ نگاری)
keyPoints: **Dialogue** (مکالمہ) must show clear conversation between two or more characters with proper speech marks and attribution
Each speaker should have a **distinct voice** with appropriate vocabulary, tone and speaking style that reflects their character
Use **natural conversational language** with appropriate interruptions, hesitations and informal expressions where suitable
Include **stage directions** in brackets to show actions, emotions or setting details that enhance the dialogue
Maintain **logical flow** where each response connects meaningfully to the previous speaker's words
Show **character development** through what characters say and how they express themselves
Use **appropriate register** - formal/informal language depending on the relationship between speakers and context
Include **conflict or tension** to make the dialogue engaging and purposeful rather than mere exchange of information
End with **clear resolution** or conclusion that feels natural and complete

definitions.definition: A written conversation between two or more characters that reveals personality, advances plot or explores themes
Instructions in brackets describing character actions, emotions or setting details during dialogue
The level of formality in language use, ranging from very formal to very informal depending on context and relationship
The unique way each character speaks, including their vocabulary choices, sentence structure and speaking patterns

howToAnswer.instruction: Create conversation with speech marks, clear speaker attribution, natural flow and distinct character voices
Use specific word choices, speaking patterns and responses that reveal personality traits

modelAnswer.question: دو دوستوں کے درمیان ایک مکالمہ لکھیں جو امتحان کی تیاری کے بارے میں بات کر رہے ہیں۔ (Write a dialogue between two friends discussing exam preparation.)
modelAnswer.answer:
احمد: "یار علی، کیا حال ہے؟ امتحان کی تیاری کیسی چل رہی ہے؟"

علی: (پریشان ہو کر) "بہت برا حال ہے بھائی۔ اردو کا پیپر بہت مشکل لگ رہا ہے۔"

احمد: "کیوں؟ کیا مسئلہ ہے؟ مکالمہ نگاری میں؟"

علی: "ہاں یار، سمجھ نہیں آتا کہ کرداروں کو مختلف آوازیں کیسے دوں۔"

احمد: "دیکھ، یہ آسان ہے۔ ہر کردار کی اپنی شخصیت ہونی چاہیے۔ جیسے بزرگ رسمی زبان استعمال کریں گے۔"

علی: (دلچسپی سے) "واقعی؟ اور بچے؟"

احمد: "بچے آسان اور سادہ الفاظ بولیں گے۔ میں تمہیں مدد کروں گا۔"

علی: (خوش ہو کر) "شکریہ یار! تم واقعی اچھے دوست ہو۔"
markBreakdown: Mark 1-2: Clear dialogue format with proper speech marks
Mark 3-4: Distinct character voices and personalities
Mark 5-6: Natural conversational flow and responses
Mark 7-8: Appropriate stage directions and emotional indicators
Mark 9-10: Complete conversation with logical conclusion

commonMistakes: Never write dialogue without speech marks - always use quotation marks to show spoken words
Students often make all characters sound the same - each speaker must have distinct vocabulary and tone
Avoid writing dialogue that sounds like written essays - use natural, conversational language with contractions
Never forget speaker attribution - clearly show who is speaking each line
Students often write dialogue without purpose - ensure conversation has clear direction and meaning
examinerTips: Dialogue writing appears regularly in Paper 2 composition section
Always plan character personalities before writing to ensure distinct voices
Use stage directions sparingly but effectively to enhance understanding
Practice writing dialogue in different scenarios - formal/informal, conflict/agreement
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: No specific diagram required, but proper dialogue format with quotation marks, speaker names, and stage directions in brackets should be clearly structured
